items.title: Black Forest Labs launched FLUX 3
items.oneliner: FLUX 3 generates 20-second video with native audio. Multimodal world models are moving from demos toward robotics.

items.title: FLUX-mimic learned factory tasks quickly
items.oneliner: Black Forest says FLUX-mimic learns factory tasks from roughly 30 minutes of demos. Data-efficient robot training just got more credible.

items.title: OpenAI launched Health in ChatGPT
items.oneliner: OpenAI added medical-record ingestion and Apple Health connections for US users. ChatGPT is entering a regulated, high-trust workflow.

items.title: Cloud infrastructure became a kinetic target
items.oneliner: IRGC claimed it struck AWS's Bahrain data center. Cloud resilience now includes geopolitics, not just redundancy diagrams.

items.title: Startups pushed back on China model limits
items.oneliner: Startup founders urged Washington not to block Chinese open-weight AI. Policy now directly shapes which models builders can ship.

items.title: Patreon cut staff over AI disruption
items.oneliner: Patreon laid off 20% of staff, citing AI's impact on software work. The labor reset is no longer theoretical.

items.title: AMD unveiled its Instinct MI455X
items.oneliner: AMD's MI455X packs 432 GB of HBM4 and scales to 72 GPUs per pod. Training clusters are becoming rack products.

items.title: Hetzner quietly tested LLM inference
items.oneliner: Hetzner opened an OpenAI-compatible inference API showing 153 ms median latency. European cloud competition is finally getting concrete.

items.title: Etched raised money for inference clusters
items.oneliner: Etched raised $300 million at a $10.3 billion valuation to build inference clusters. Serving models is now venture-scale infrastructure.

items.title: Varta filed for insolvency in Germany
items.oneliner: Varta filed for insolvency, exposing Europe's battery squeeze. Hardware supply chains remain the hidden dependency behind every AI roadmap.
